IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: THE MUSEUM PRESERVES, EDUCATES AND AMPLIFIES THE DIVERSE CONTRIBUTIONS AND VOICES OF WOMEN.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE NATIONAL WOMEN'S HISTORY MUSEUM IS A NONPARTISAN ORGANIZATION THAT PRESERVES, EDUCATES AND AMPLIFIES THE DIVERSE CONTRIBUTIONS AND VOICES OF WOMEN AND IS A LEADING EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION DEDICATED TO CELEBRATING WOMEN'S CONTRIBUTIONS TO OUR NATION'S SOCIAL, CULTURAL AND ECONOMIC LIFE.
IRS990.Desc: AMERICA 250: IN JUNE 2024, THE NATIONAL WOMEN'S HISTORY MUSEUM WAS SELECTED AS THE NATIONAL RESOURCE PARTNER FOR WOMEN'S HISTORY FOR OUR COUNTRY'S UPCOMING SEMI-QUINCENTENNIAL. AS A NATIONAL RESOURCE PARTNER, THE MUSEUM WILL HELP AMPLIFY AND SHARE THE HISTORY THAT WOMEN HAVE CONTRIBUTED TO OVER THE LAST 250 YEARS OF OUR NATION'S HISTORY. THE MUSEUM LAUNCHED A UNIFYING CAMPAIGN IN MARCH 2025 CALLED "SHE IS NOT A FOOTNOTE" TO BRING TOGETHER ORGANIZATIONS THAT SUPPORT WOMEN AND GIRLS AHEAD OF THE JULY 2026 COMMEMORATION. THE MUSEUM WILL ALSO SPONSOR ITS OWN INDIVIDUAL PROJECTS FOR THIS COMMEMORATION, INCLUDING ACTIVATING A NEW YOUTH COUNCIL ON COLLEGE CAMPUSES TO PLAY WOMEN'S HISTORY TRIVIA GAMES WITH THE NEW GAME PUBLISHED IN NOV 2025 BY THE NWHM CALLED "WOMEN'S HISTORY PLUNGE".

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING: THE MUSEUM CONTINUED ITS COMMITMENT TO FREE, ACCESSIBLE WOMEN'S HISTORY EDUCATION THROUGH A ROBUST VIRTUAL PROGRAMMING SCHEDULE THAT INCLUDED ONLINE EXHIBITIONS, A COMPENDIUM OF BIOGRAPHIES, CLASSROOM-READY LESSON PLANS, RESEARCH RESOURCES, VIRTUAL FIELD TRIPS, A VIRTUAL BOOK CLUB, PANEL DISCUSSIONS, AUTHOR TALKS AND VIRTUAL WORKSHOPS FOR EDUCATORS' PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT. THE MUSEUM'S OPEN RESOURCE, EDUCATIONAL WEBSITE AVERAGED OVER 6 MILLION VISITORS FROM AROUND THE GLOBE IN 2025. IN 2025, WE CONCLUDED OUR WORK WITH THE US MINT AS THE FINAL QUARTERS FEATURING WOMEN WERE LAUNCHED. IN 2025, THE MUSEUM DEVELOPED, CO-HOSTED AND/OR SPEARHEADED SEVERAL KEY COLLABORATIVE PROJECTS AND EVENTS WITH NEW STAKEHOLDERS AND LEADING VOICES IN WOMEN'S ADVOCACY AND EMPOWERMENT SPACES. THIS CONCLUDES OUR MULTI-YEAR PARTNERSHIP WITH THE U.S. MINT AND THE SMITHSONIAN INSTITUTION AMERICAN WOMEN'S HISTORY INITIATIVE TO HIGHLIGHT THE AMERICAN WOMEN'S QUARTERS PROGRAM.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: EXHIBITIONS: IN 2024, THE MUSEUM RECEIVED A PLANNING GRANT FROM THE LILLY FOUNDATION TO DEVELOP AN EXHIBIT CALLED "FAITH AND FREEDOM," TO ILLUMINATE THE PROFOUND INFLUENCE OF FAITH AND RELIGION ON AMERICAN WOMEN. THIS EXHIBIT CONCEPT RECEIVED FULL FUNDING FROM THE LILLY FOUNDATION IN DECEMBER 2024 AND WAS FULLY DEVELOPED IN 2025. THE MUSEUM ANTICIPATES OPENING THE NEW EXHIBITION, OFFICIALLY CALLED: "UNTIL WE ARE ALL FREE: WOMEN, FAITH, AND SOCIAL JUSTICE" IN JANUARY 2026 IN DALLAS, TX.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: OTHER - 2021 AMOUNT: $ 0. 2022 AMOUNT: $ 0. 2023 AMOUNT: $ 50. 2024 AMOUNT: $ 0. 2025 AMOUNT: $ 0.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: COLLECTIONS INCLUDE PANELS, HISTORICAL BOOKS, PAPERS AND MEMORABILIA. ALL COLLECTIONS ARE HELD FOR PUBLIC EXHIBITION, EDUCATION OR RESEARCH. EACH OF THE ITEMS IS CATALOGUED, PRESERVED AND CARED FOR, AND ACTIVITIES VERIFYING THEIR EXISTENCE AND ASSESSING THEIR CONDITION ARE CONTINUOUSLY PERFORMED. THE COLLECTIONS ARE NOT RECOGNIZED AS ASSETS IN THE AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. PURCHASES OF COLLECTION ITEMS ARE RECORDED AS DECREASES IN NET ASSETS WITHOUT DONOR RESTRICTIONS IN THE YEAR IN WHICH THE ITEMS ARE ACQUIRED. PURCHASES OF COLLECTION ITEMS ARE RECORDED AS DECREASES IN NET ASSETS WITH DONOR RESTRICTIONS IF THE ASSETS USED TO PURCHASE THE ITEMS ARE RESTRICTED BY DONORS.
THE COLLECTION IS MADE UP OF HISTORICAL TREASURES AND ASSETS THAT ARE HELD FOR PUBLIC EXHIBITION, EDUCATION, OR RESEARCH ALL FOR THE PURPOSE OF PUBLIC AWARENESS.

THE MUSEUM PERFORMED AN EVALUATION OF UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2025, AND DETERMINED THAT THERE ARE NO MATTERS THAT WOULD REQUIRE RECOGNITION IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS OR THAT MAY HAVE ANY EFFECT ON ITS TAX-EXEMPT STATUS.
ADDITIONAL FUNDRAISING EVENT EXPENSES 368,149. COST OF GOODS SOLD 6,811.
FUNDRAISING EVENT EXPENSES 368,149. COST OF GOODS SOLD 6,811.
DURING THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2025, JENNIFER HERRERA WALPOLE, VP, EXTERNAL AFFAIRS UNTIL MAY OF 2025, RECEIVED A SEVERANCE PAYMENT OF $48,067.
THE ORGANIZATION REPORTS THE NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS IN PART I, COLUMN (B).
SOLICITATION OF AUCTION ITEMS DONE THROUGH LYDIA FENET LLC .

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FINANCE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD REVIEWS THE 990 AS DOES THE AUDIT COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD. BEFORE THE FEDERAL FORM 990 IS FILED WITH THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE, THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS RECEIVE A COPY OF THE 990 FOR REVIEW AND HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY TO DIRECT CHANGES IF NECESSARY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MUSEUM MONITORS AND ENFORCES COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY BY HOLDING DISCUSSIONS ON CONFLICTS AT BOARD MEETINGS AND STAFF MEETINGS. ADDITIONALLY, THE BOARD MEMBERS DISCLOSE IN WRITING THAT THERE ARE NO CONFLICTS OF INTEREST OR DISCLOSE ALL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST EACH YEAR. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS DETERMINES HOW TO HANDLE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST ON A CASE BY CASE BASIS, BUT IN THE EVENT OF A CONFLICT THE BOARD MEMBER(S) WITH THE CONFLICT WOULD BE RECUSED FROM VOTING ON THE MATTER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE, USING COMPARABLE COMPENSATION DATA AND FORMAL INPUT FROM THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, REVIEWS AND APPROVES THE PRESIDENT & CEO'S COMPENSATION DURING AN EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE MEETING. THE DECISION IS DOCUMENTED IN WRITING AND COMMUNICATED IN WRITING AND VERBALLY TO THE PRESIDENT & CEO. THE CEO COMPENSATION WAS REVIEWED IN 2025.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MUSEUM MAKES THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

GRANT SUPPORT / EXHIBIT PLANNING: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 253,995. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 253,995.
